reason: promql-parser silently parses an astronomically large decimal literal as +Inf instead of erroring like Go's strconv.ParseFloat ('value out of range'); numeric-literal overflow validation is not one of the M2 proof-subset constructs (features.md §3) — a lexer numeric-fidelity edge belonging to full M6 compliance.

promql-parser 0.10.0 unconditionally reserves the identifiers 'anchored'/'smoothed' as keyword tokens (T_ANCHORED/T_SMOOTHED in token.rs, declared %expect-unused in promql.y — forward-reserved for a not-yet-implemented feature), so they cannot be used as ordinary metric/label names even though Prometheus v3.13 does not reserve them. Patch: drop 'anchored'/'smoothed' from token.rs's unconditional KEYWORDS map (leaf lexing, no grammar production touched).

promql-parser 0.10.0 unconditionally reserves the identifiers 'anchored'/'smoothed' as keyword tokens (T_ANCHORED/T_SMOOTHED in token.rs, declared %expect-unused in promql.y — forward-reserved for a not-yet-implemented feature), so they cannot be used as ordinary metric/label names even though Prometheus v3.13 does not reserve them. Patch: drop 'anchored'/'smoothed' from token.rs's unconditional KEYWORDS map (leaf lexing, no grammar production touched).

same 'anchored'/'smoothed' reserved-keyword bug as the bare-metric-selector case, manifesting inside a by(...) grouping list. Patch: drop 'anchored'/'smoothed' from token.rs's unconditional KEYWORDS map (leaf lexing).

promql-parser's lexer raises 'unknown escape sequence' for a literal backslash-quote sequence inside a backtick (raw) string literal, even though backtick strings apply no escape processing at all (confirmed directly: `a\nb` round-trips as the literal two characters '\n', but `a\"b` errors). Leaf lexer state bug in the raw-string scanning rule, not a grammar production; string literals underlie every matcher value in the M2 subset.

promql-parser's duration lexer accepts a bare number without a unit suffix wherever Prometheus's base grammar requires one (confirmed directly: 'foo offset 5' parses as a 5s offset, and 'foo[5]' as a 5s range) — upstream's rejection here ('duration out of range') is reached only via its experimental duration-expression parser, but the crate's own unconditional bare-number acceptance is a real, always-on divergence from the base v3.13 grammar, reachable through 'offset' and the matrix-selector range '[...]', both directly in the M2 subset. Patch: require a duration unit suffix on bare number literals in the duration-literal lexer rule (leaf lexing).

promql-parser's duration lexer accepts a bare number without a unit suffix wherever Prometheus's base grammar requires one (confirmed directly: 'foo offset 5' parses as a 5s offset, and 'foo[5]' as a 5s range) — upstream's rejection here ('duration out of range') is reached only via its experimental duration-expression parser, but the crate's own unconditional bare-number acceptance is a real, always-on divergence from the base v3.13 grammar, reachable through 'offset' and the matrix-selector range '[...]', both directly in the M2 subset. Patch: require a duration unit suffix on bare number literals in the duration-literal lexer rule (leaf lexing).

'and'/'or'/'unless' vector set operators are M6 parity (features.md §3 'Other: ... set operators'), not part of the M2 binary-op subset (arithmetic/comparison with bool/on/ignoring). Display drops an explicit empty ignoring() modifier when re-rendering, changing the modifier field on reparse.
@ lands M3, out of the M2 proof subset regardless of the Display round-trip outcome (Display renders a negative @ timestamp as '0.000').

single-quoted label names/values are the UTF-8-quoted-label-name feature, M6-only per features.md §3.

promql-parser's Display re-serializes a selector's Matchers list in alphabetical-by-name order rather than parse-preserved order, so a round-trip through Display+parse changes matcher order and fails strict AST PartialEq (order-sensitive Vec) even though the *set* of matchers is unchanged. Every M2 selector with 2+ matchers of mixed types hits this. Patch: make Display preserve insertion order (leaf Display fix, not a grammar production).

Display collapses an explicit empty 'by()' grouping clause to no modifier at all, so round-trip parse produces an AggregateExpr with modifier=None instead of Some(Include([])) — an AST-shape difference on one of the M2-subset's explicitly enumerated constructs ('aggregations ... with by/without'). Leaf Display fix: render an explicit empty by()/without() rather than omitting the clause.
nested subqueries ([5m:], [4m:3s]) and @ are both M6/M3-only per features.md §3.
info() is out of the M2 function list; the round-trip failure here is again matcher-order Display drift, inside an out-of-scope construct.
